question: Does the image depict a realistic no-code automation workflow editor web UI with a multi-panel layout (top bar, left palette, center canvas, right properties panel, bottom run log)?
Does the top section contain breadcrumb navigation and a save/publish button bar?
Does the left sidebar display a component palette with categorized collapsible sections and draggable items?
Does the central canvas contain a complex flowchart with distinct 'Start' nodes and multiple action nodes?
Does the flowchart include conditional decision nodes represented as diamond shapes?
Does the flowchart visually demonstrate parallel branches (one node splitting into multiple paths)?
Does the flowchart visually demonstrate loop circuits (connectors cycling back to previous nodes)?
Are the nodes in the flowchart connected by arrowed lines indicating flow direction?
Are there distinct connector styles visible (specifically both solid and dashed lines) within the flowchart?
Is there a mini-map thumbnail located specifically in the bottom-right corner of the central canvas area?
Does the right-side properties panel contain accordion-style collapsible sections?
Do the accordion section titles in the right panel include 'Input', 'Output', 'Retry Strategy', or 'Log Level'?
Does the properties panel contain UI elements like toggles, dropdowns, and text field placeholders?
Is there an expandable run log table in the bottom section?
Does the run log table include headers for 'Time', 'Step', 'Status', 'Duration', and 'Details'?
Is there a horizontal step progress bar with numbered dots (1 to N) located above the run log table?
Are the geometric shapes (rectangles, diamonds) and UI borders straight and defined, without significant distortion?
